narrative:
DRIVER
USD leads D1 CSM at 100 but is fading hard on H4 to 44, a 56-point divergence that is the single most important signal in this brief — this is a USD rollover in progress, not a trend continuation. CHF H4 CSM accelerates to 100 versus D1 at 33, AUD surges to H4 70 versus D1 at 31, and NZD hits H4 62 versus D1 at 24, meaning USDCHF, AUDUSD, and NZDUSD are all expressing the same rotation out of USD into safe and commodity proxies simultaneously. AUDUSD and NZDUSD carry a 0.88 correlation confirming the move is structural, while AUDJPY at 0.90 correlation to AUDUSD amplifies AUD strength into the JPY cross where GBPJPY D1 plus H4 both read buy, creating a rare confirmed long on that pair.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: None
Fading: None

WATCH
USDCHF breakdown below current H4 structure triggers continued CHF acceleration and confirms USD fade extends to EURUSD short squeeze toward nearest D1 resistance.
AUDUSD reclaim of H4 neutral zone confirms AUD H4 CSM momentum and opens extension toward D1 resistance with NZDUSD following given 0.88 correlation.
USDCAD D1 strong buy plus H4 buy signals diverge against USD fade theme — a CAD CSM collapse at zero H4 means USDCAD is the one USD long that holds if USD stabilises.

themes.theme: Equity market strength and risk-on sentiment
Geopolitical risk reduction from Iran peace hopes
Fed policy uncertainty under Kevin Warsh

key_observation: Headlines predominantly reflect equity market strength and risk appetite rather than FX-specific drivers. S&P 500 strength and tech sector gains support risk-on positioning. Limited direct currency implications beyond typical risk-on USD weakness offset by equity market optimism. Iran peace hopes and institutional equity positioning dominate narrative.
watch: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh policy signals, continued equity market momentum, geopolitical developments on Iran, tech sector earnings sustainability
